title: Case Study
description: A narrative case study describing a real or illustrative deployment, for website publication and discovery. This schema is also used as-is for the 'tool' content kind (content/tools/*.json) - an interactive demonstrator is presented the same editorial way as a case study (narrative, classification, maturity), so rather than maintain a near-duplicate schema, a tool validates against this one verbatim and is distinguished only by which folder it lives in. A tool's launch/demo link is the same field a case study already uses for its external link: classification.site.project_link. Aligned v2: the bespoke 'discoverability' object was replaced by the shared 'classification' block (plus top-level language/reading_time/maturity), and the relation vocabulary was unified (satisfies -> demonstrates, related_case_study -> related, added 'instantiates' for use-case linkage).

title.description: Headline written for a website reader (not an internal codename).

narrative.description: The full case study body. Supports Markdown (headings, images, lists, links, emphasis).

maturity.description: Deployment maturity of the specific case described (promoted from the old discoverability.maturity to top level, shared with use cases).

hero_image.description: Path or URL to the primary image, used in listing cards and social-share previews.

reading_time_minutes.description: Estimated reading time shown to the visitor.

classification.description: Replaces the former bespoke 'discoverability' object. Map: sector -> market_segments (+ free facets), technology -> eo_techniques, organisation_type -> organisation_types, outcome_type -> outcome_types, plus the new 'products' facet.

key_results.description: Short, demonstrated outcome statements for pull-quotes / stat callouts (the use-case analogue is 'benefits', which are expected).

tags.description: Free-text keyword tags, independent of the structured classification facets.

relations.description: Typed links. Key edges: 'instantiates' -> use_case (this case realises that pattern), 'illustrates' -> need, 'demonstrates' -> requirement, plus 'depends_on', 'related', 'supersedes'.

stakeholders.description: Real-world people, organisations, or roles featured in the story (not abstract personas).
